title: Find K-th smallest element in BST

content.problemStatement:
Given the root node of a binary search tree (BST) and an integer k.


Return the kth smallest and largest value (1-indexed) of all values of the nodes in the tree.



Return the 1st integer as kth smallest and 2nd integer as kth largest in the returned array.
constraints: 1 <= k <= number of nodes in the binary search tree
The binary search tree will have at most 10^4 nodes
Each node in the binary search tree will have a unique value between 1 and 10^4
content.inputFormat: The input will contain the root of a binary search tree and an integer k.
content.outputFormat: The output will be an array of two integers, where the first integer is the kth smallest value and the second integer is the kth largest value in the binary search tree.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
The 1st smallest value in the given BST is 1.  
The 1st largest value in the given BST is 4.

**Example 2:**

The 3rd smallest value in the given BST is 3.  
The 3rd largest value in the given BST is 3.
        
    



**Disclaimer**: Here is the practice link to help you assess your knowledge better. It's highly recommend trying to solve it before looking at the solution.

solution.approach: Perform an in-order traversal of the binary search tree to get the values in ascending order, then find the kth smallest and kth largest values.

code.Python3:
class TreeNode:
    def __init__(self, val=0, left=None, right=None):
        self.data = val
        self.left = left
        self.right = right

class Solution:
    def kLargesSmall(self, root, k):
        # Helper function to perform an in-order traversal of the BST
        def inorder_traversal(node, values):
            if node:
                inorder_traversal(node.left, values)
                values.append(node.data)
                inorder_traversal(node.right, values)
        
        # List to store the node values
        values = []
        # Perform in-order traversal and collect values
        inorder_traversal(root, values)
        
        # Find the kth smallest and kth largest values
        kth_smallest = values[k - 1]
        kth_largest = values[-k]
        
        return [kth_smallest, kth_largest]
